Constantinople 1453 — Component Design

Unit & Leader Token Template

Standard 5/8″ (16mm) square line-unit counters and circular leader tokens, styled to match the Event/Ops card set. Faction color is the primary read at a glance; icon marks unit class; corner boxes carry Attack/Defense. Multi-step units flip to a striped reverse at reduced strength — same color family, lower stats, no separate token to lose.

Production Notes

Sizing, print, and convention notes for the counter sheet.
Counter size
5/8″ (16mm) square — the GMT standard for line units (used across the COIN series and most GMT wargames). Large enough to hold an icon, name, and two stat boxes at arm's length; small enough to keep 100+ counters on two sheets. Shown here at 108px for legibility; export at 187×187px per counter (300dpi).
Leader tokens
Same 5/8″ footprint but circular, with a gold ring border — the shape swap alone reads "unique/named unit" on the table without needing a legend. Keeps them on the same die-cut sheet as the squares (most counter manufacturers cut mixed square/circle sheets without a surcharge). All 8 statted leaders from DESIGN.md §6.2 are included; the badge shows their flat combat bonus (red = Attack, navy = Defense, matching the unit stat-box convention) rather than an abstract rating. Giustiniani and Girolamo Minotto — the two foreign-contingent commanders — get the same purple/pale-gold and purple/teal diagonal split as their Genoese and Venetian troops; Constantine XI and Loukas Notaras stay plain purple as native Byzantine commanders.
Corners
Sharp, unrounded corners — classic GMT convention (rounding is more of a mainstream/Euro-game tell). A 1px hairline inset frame (.counter::after) does the "printed edge" job instead.
Stat boxes
Bottom-left = Attack (crimson), bottom-right = Defense (navy) — consistent across every unit regardless of faction color, so the eye always finds Atk/Def in the same two corners.
Reduced step (flip side)
Same base gradient as the full-strength face, with one lighter horizontal band crossing the middle (pale-gold for Genoese, a translucent white band elsewhere) — reads as "this unit" at a glance, but visibly weakened, without the busier repeating-stripe look. Stats drop, icon and name stay put. One physical counter carries both steps; losing a step is a flip, not a removal, until the reduced side is also eliminated.
Counter sheet layout
Standard sheets run 17×13 counters at 5/8″ (a ~176×176mm die-cut sheet, the common commercial size). At ~30–40 unique units × front/back, plan on one full sheet plus a partial second for leaders and markers.
Arabic sword (traced) — Bashi-bazouk, unpaid irregular volunteers.
Turban (traced) — Azap, paid irregular infantry.
Börk cap (traced, plain) — New Levy Janissary, conscripted standing corps.
Börk cap (traced) — Janissary, elite standing corps.
Plumed börk (traced) — Solak, the Sultan's personal bodyguard archers, known for their tall aigrette-topped cap.
Crossed spears (traced) — Byzantine Militia, native levies and armed citizenry.
Crossbow (traced) — Giustiniani's Company, his professional condottieri.
Sword (traced) — Genoese Garrison, broader Genoese troops in the city's service.
Anchor (traced) — Venetian Marines, crews and soldiers off Minotto's ships.
Bow & arrow (traced) — Cretan Sailors, historically renowned archers. Recolored to the Venetian palette — both are the city's Italian-allied naval contingents.
Crossed keys (traced) — Isidore's Contingent, the Cardinal's ~200 men (proposed addition, not yet in DESIGN.md §6.1).

Two-tone diagonal split (Giustiniani's Company, Genoese Garrison, Venetian Marines, Cretan Sailors, Isidore's Contingent) marks a unit as an allied foreign contingent rather than native Byzantine — purple stays constant as the Byzantine-side color, the second color is shared by allies from the same power (Genoa's pale gold, Venice's teal — Cretan Sailors take the same teal as the Venetians, since both were the city's Italian-allied naval arm — and the Papacy's milky white), so each reads as "allied, and specifically which ally" at a glance.

Note: reduced-side (flip) stats shown are illustrative placeholders — DESIGN.md §6.1 specifies front-side Attack/Defense and step counts, but not exact reduced values yet; treat those numbers as a starting point for playtesting, not a final figure.
